fields.name: IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED
fields.description: IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED .

fields.name: IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED
fields.description: IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED .

fields.name: IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED
fields.description: IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED .

fields.name: IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED
fields.description: IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED .

fields.name: IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED
fields.description: IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED .

fields.name: IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED
fields.description: IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED .

name: CCSIDR
description: Provides information about the architecture of the currently selected cache. When FEAT_CCIDX is implemented, this register is used in conjunction with CCSIDR2 .

fields.name: RES0
fields.description: Reserved, RES0 .

fields.name: Associativity
fields.description: (Associativity of cache) - 1, therefore a value of 0 indicates an associativity of 1. The associativity does not have to be a power of 2.

fields.name: LineSize
fields.description: (Log 2 (Number of bytes in cache line)) - 4. For example: For a line length of 16 bytes: Log 2 (16) = 4, LineSize entry = 0. This is the minimum line length. For a line length of 32 bytes: Log 2 (32) = 5, LineSize entry = 1. The C++ 17 specification has two defined parameters relating to the granularity of memory that does not interfere. For generic software and tools, Arm will set the hardware_destructive_interference_size parameter to 256 bytes and the hardware_constructive_interference_size parameter to 64 bytes.

name: CLIDR
description: Identifies the type of cache, or caches, that are implemented at each level and can be managed using the architected cache maintenance instructions that operate by set/way, up to a maximum of seven levels. Also identifies the Level of Coherence (LoC) and Level of Unification (LoU) for the cache hierarchy.

fields.name: ICB
fields.description: Inner cache boundary. This field indicates the boundary for caching Inner Cacheable memory regions.

values.label: Not disclosed by this mechanism.
L1 cache is the highest Inner Cacheable level.
L2 cache is the highest Inner Cacheable level.
L3 cache is the highest Inner Cacheable level.

fields.name: LoUU
fields.description: Level of Unification Uniprocessor for the cache hierarchy. For a description of the values of this field, see Terminology for Clean, Invalidate, and Clean and Invalidate instructions . This field does not describe the requirements for instruction cache invalidation. See CTR .DIC. When FEAT_S2FWB is implemented, the architecture requires that this field is zero so that no levels of data cache need to be cleaned in order to manage coherency with instruction fetches.

fields.name: LoC
fields.description: Level of Coherence for the cache hierarchy. For a description of the values of this field, see Terminology for Clean, Invalidate, and Clean and Invalidate instructions .

fields.name: LoUIS
fields.description: Level of Unification Inner Shareable for the cache hierarchy. For a description of the values of this field, see Terminology for Clean, Invalidate, and Clean and Invalidate instructions . This field does not describe the requirements for instruction cache invalidation. See CTR .DIC. When FEAT_S2FWB is implemented, the architecture requires that this field is zero so that no levels of data cache need to be cleaned in order to manage coherency with instruction fetches.

fields.name: Ctype<n>
fields.description: Cache Type fields. Indicate the type of cache that is implemented and can be managed using the architected cache maintenance instructions that operate by set/way at each level, from Level 1 up to a maximum of seven levels of cache hierarchy.

values.label: No cache.
Instruction cache only.
Data cache only.
Separate instruction and data caches.
Unified cache.

name: CNTFRQ
description: This register is provided so that software can discover the effective frequency of the system counter. It must be programmed with this value as part of system initialization. The value of the register is not interpreted by hardware.

fields.name: ClockFreq
fields.description: Clock frequency. Indicates the effective frequency of the system counter, in Hz.

name: CNTHCTL
description: Controls the generation of an event stream from the physical counter, and access from Non-secure EL1 modes to the physical counter and the Non-secure EL1 physical timer.

fields.name: RES0
fields.description: Reserved, RES0 .

fields.name: EVNTIS
fields.description: Controls the scale of the generation of the event stream.

values.label: The CNTHCTL.EVNTI field applies to CNTPCT [15:0].
The CNTHCTL.EVNTI field applies to CNTPCT [23:8].

fields.name: RES0
fields.description: Reserved, RES0 .

fields.name: RES0
fields.description: Reserved, RES0 .

fields.name: EVNTI
fields.description: Selects which bit of CNTPCT , as seen from EL2, is the trigger for the event stream generated from that counter when that stream is enabled. If FEAT_ECV is implemented, and CNTHCTL.EVNTIS is 1, this field selects a trigger bit in the range 8 to 23 of CNTPCT . Otherwise, this field selects a trigger bit in the range 0 to 15 of CNTPCT .

fields.name: EVNTDIR
fields.description: Controls which transition of the CNTPCT trigger bit, as seen from EL2 and defined by EVNTI, generates an event when the event stream is enabled.

values.label: A 0 to 1 transition of the trigger bit triggers an event.
A 1 to 0 transition of the trigger bit triggers an event.

fields.name: EVNTEN
fields.description: Enables the generation of an event stream from CNTPCT as seen from EL2.

values.label: Disables the event stream.
Enables the event stream.

fields.name: PL1PCEN
fields.description: Traps Non-secure EL0 and EL1 MRC or MCR accesses, reported using EC syndrome value 0x03 , and MRRC or MCRR accesses, reported using EC syndrome value 0x04 , to the physical timer registers to Hyp mode.

values.label: Non-secure EL0 and EL1 accesses to the CNTP_CTL , CNTP_CVAL , and CNTP_TVAL are trapped to Hyp mode, unless trapped by CNTKCTL .PL0PTEN.
This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.

fields.name: PL1PCTEN
fields.description: Traps Non-secure EL0 and EL1 MRRC or MCRR accesses, reported using EC syndrome value 0x04 , to the physical counter register to Hyp mode.

values.label: Non-secure EL0 and EL1 accesses to the CNTPCT are trapped to Hyp mode, unless it is trapped by CNTKCTL .PL0PCTEN.
This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.

name: CNTHP_CTL
long_name: Counter-timer Hyp Physical Timer Control register
description: Control register for the Hyp mode physical timer.

fields.name: RES0
fields.description: Reserved, RES0 .

fields.name: ISTATUS
fields.description: The status of the timer. This bit indicates whether the timer condition is met:

values.label: Timer condition is not met.
Timer condition is met.

fields.name: IMASK
fields.description: Timer interrupt mask bit. Permitted values are:

values.label: Timer interrupt is not masked by the IMASK bit.
Timer interrupt is masked by the IMASK bit.

fields.name: ENABLE
fields.description: Enables the timer. Permitted values are:

values.label: Timer disabled.
Timer enabled.

name: CNTHP_CVAL
description: Holds the compare value for the Hyp mode physical timer.

fields.name: CompareValue
fields.description: Holds the EL2 physical timer CompareValue. When CNTHP_CTL .ENABLE is 1, the timer condition is met when ( CNTPCT - CompareValue) is greater than or equal to zero. This means that CompareValue acts like a 64-bit upcounter timer. When the timer condition is met: CNTHP_CTL .ISTATUS is set to 1. If CNTHP_CTL .IMASK is 0, an interrupt is generated. When CNTHP_CTL .ENABLE is 0, the timer condition is not met, but CNTPCT continues to count. If the Generic counter is implemented at a size less than 64 bits, then this field is permitted to be implemented at the same width as the counter, and the upper bits are RES0 . The value of this field is treated as zero-extended in all counter calculations.

name: CNTHP_TVAL
long_name: Counter-timer Hyp Physical Timer TimerValue register
description: Holds the timer value for the Hyp mode physical timer.

fields.name: TimerValue
fields.description: The TimerValue view of the EL2 physical timer. On a read of this register: If CNTHP_CTL .ENABLE is 0, the value returned is UNKNOWN . If CNTHP_CTL .ENABLE is 1, the value returned is ( CNTHP_CVAL - CNTPCT ). On a write of this register, CNTHP_CVAL is set to ( CNTPCT + TimerValue), where TimerValue is treated as a signed 32-bit integer. When CNTHP_CTL .ENABLE is 1, the timer condition is met when ( CNTPCT - CNTHP_CVAL ) is greater than or equal to zero. This means that TimerValue acts like a 32-bit downcounter timer. When the timer condition is met: CNTHP_CTL .ISTATUS is set to 1. If CNTHP_CTL .IMASK is 0, an interrupt is generated. When CNTHP_CTL .ENABLE is 0, the TimerValue cannot be read but continues to decrement. When the timer is enabled, the TimerValue represents the elapsed time whether that time was spent enabled or disabled.

name: CNTKCTL
description: Controls the generation of an event stream from the virtual counter, and access from EL0 modes to the physical counter, virtual counter, EL1 physical timers, and the virtual timer.

fields.name: RES0
fields.description: Reserved, RES0 .

fields.name: EVNTIS
fields.description: Controls the scale of the generation of the event stream.

values.label: The CNTKCTL.EVNTI field applies to CNTVCT [15:0].
The CNTKCTL.EVNTI field applies to CNTVCT [23:8].

fields.name: RES0
fields.description: Reserved, RES0 .

fields.name: RES0
fields.description: Reserved, RES0 .

fields.name: PL0PTEN
fields.description: Traps PL0 accesses to the physical timer registers to Undefined mode.

values.label: PL0 accesses to the CNTP_CTL , CNTP_CVAL , and CNTP_TVAL registers are trapped to Undefined mode.
This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.

fields.name: PL0VTEN
fields.description: Traps PL0 accesses to the virtual timer registers to Undefined mode.

values.label: PL0 accesses to the CNTV_CTL , CNTV_CVAL , and CNTV_TVAL registers are trapped to Undefined mode.
This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.

fields.name: EVNTI
fields.description: Selects which bit of CNTVCT , as seen from EL1, is the trigger for the event stream generated from that counter when that stream is enabled. If FEAT_ECV is implemented, and CNTKCTL.EVNTIS is 1, this field selects a trigger bit in the range 8 to 23 of CNTVCT . Otherwise, this field selects a trigger bit in the range 0 to 15 of CNTVCT .

fields.name: EVNTDIR
fields.description: Controls which transition of the CNTVCT trigger bit, as seen from EL1 and defined by EVNTI, generates an event when the event stream is enabled.

values.label: A 0 to 1 transition of the trigger bit triggers an event.
A 1 to 0 transition of the trigger bit triggers an event.

fields.name: EVNTEN
fields.description: Enables the generation of an event stream from CNTVCT as seen from EL1.

values.label: Disables the event stream.
Enables the event stream.

fields.name: PL0VCTEN
fields.description: Traps PL0 accesses to the frequency register and virtual counter register to Undefined mode.

values.label: PL0 accesses to the CNTVCT are trapped to Undefined mode. PL0 accesses to the CNTFRQ register are trapped to Undefined mode, if CNTKCTL .PL0PCTEN is also 0.
This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.

fields.name: PL0PCTEN
fields.description: Traps PL0 accesses to the frequency register and physical counter register to Undefined mode.

values.label: PL0 accesses to the CNTPCT are trapped to Undefined mode. PL0 accesses to the CNTFRQ register are trapped to Undefined mode, if CNTKCTL .PL0VCTEN is also 0.
This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.

description: Holds the 64-bit physical count value.

description: Holds the 64-bit virtual count value. The virtual count value is equal to the physical count value minus the virtual offset visible in CNTVOFF .

fields.name: VirtualCount
fields.description: Virtual count value.

name: CNTVOFF
description: Holds the 64-bit virtual offset. This is the offset between the physical count value visible in CNTPCT and the virtual count value visible in CNTVCT .

fields.description: Virtual offset. If the Generic counter is implemented at a size less than 64 bits, then this field is permitted to be implemented at the same width as the counter, and the upper bits are RES0 . The value of this field is treated as zero-extended in all counter calculations.

name: CONTEXTIDR
description: Identifies the current Process Identifier and, when using the Short-descriptor translation table format, the Address Space Identifier. The value of the whole of this register is called the Context ID and is used by: The debug logic, for Linked and Unlinked Context ID matching. The trace logic, to identify the current process. The significance of this register is for debug and trace use only.

fields.name: PROCID
fields.description: Process Identifier. This field must be programmed with a unique value that identifies the current process.

fields.name: ASID
fields.description: Address Space Identifier. This field is programmed with the value of the current ASID.

name: CPACR
description: Controls access to trace, and to Advanced SIMD and floating-point functionality from EL0, EL1, and EL3. In an implementation that includes EL2, the CPACR has no effect on instructions executed at EL2.

fields.name: ASEDIS
fields.description: Disables PL0 and PL1 execution of Advanced SIMD instructions.

values.label: This control permits execution of Advanced SIMD instructions at PL0 and PL1.
All instruction encodings that are Advanced SIMD instruction encodings, but are not also floating-point instruction encodings, are UNDEFINED at PL0 and PL1.

fields.name: RES0
fields.description: Reserved, RES0 .

fields.name: TRCDIS
fields.description: Traps PL0 and PL1 System register accesses to all implemented trace registers to Undefined mode.

values.label: This control has no effect on PL0 and PL1 System register accesses to trace registers.
PL0 and PL1 System register accesses to all implemented trace registers are trapped to Undefined mode.

fields.name: RES0
fields.description: Reserved, RES0 .

fields.name: cp11
fields.description: The value of this field is ignored. If this field is programmed with a different value to the cp10 field then this field is UNKNOWN on a direct read of the CPACR. If the implementation does not include Advanced SIMD and floating-point functionality, this field is RES0 . In Non-secure state, if EL3 is implemented and is using AArch32, when the value of NSACR .cp10 is 0, this field behaves as RAZ/WI, regardless of its actual value.

fields.name: cp10
fields.description: Defines the access rights for the Advanced SIMD and floating-point functionality. Possible values of the field are:

values.label: PL0 and PL1 accesses to Advanced SIMD and floating-point registers or instructions are UNDEFINED .
PL0 accesses to Advanced SIMD and floating-point registers or instructions are UNDEFINED .

Reserved. The effect of programming this field to this value is CONSTRAINED UNPREDICTABLE choice between: The value of this field is treated as 0b00 , 0b01 , or 0b11 for all purposes other than reading the value of the field. PL1 accesses to floating-point and Advanced SIMD regsiters or instructions are UNDEFINED .
This control permits full access to the Advanced SIMD and floating-point functionality from PL0 and PL1.

fields.name: RES0
fields.description: Reserved, RES0 .

name: CSSELR
description: Selects the current Cache Size ID Register, CCSIDR , by specifying the required cache level and the cache type, which is either instruction cache or data cache. If FEAT_CCIDX is implemented, CSSELR also selects the current CCSIDR2 .

fields.name: RES0
fields.description: Reserved, RES0 .

fields.name: Level
fields.description: Cache level of required cache. Permitted values are:

values.label: Level 1 cache.
Level 2 cache.
Level 3 cache.
Level 4 cache.
Level 5 cache.
Level 6 cache.
Level 7 cache.

fields.name: InD
fields.description: Instruction not Data bit. Permitted values are:

values.label: Data or unified cache.
Instruction cache.

name: CTR
description: Provides information about the architecture of the caches.

fields.name: RES1
fields.description: Reserved, RES1 .

fields.name: RES0
fields.description: Reserved, RES0 .

fields.name: DIC
fields.description: Instruction cache invalidation requirements for data to instruction coherence.

values.label: Instruction cache invalidation to the Point of Unification is required for data to instruction coherence.
Instruction cache invalidation to the Point of Unification is not required for data to instruction coherence.

fields.name: IDC
fields.description: Data cache clean requirements for instruction to data coherence. The meaning of this bit is:

values.label: Data cache clean to the Point of Unification is required for instruction to data coherence, unless CLIDR .LoC == 0b000 or ( CLIDR .LoUIS == 0b000 and CLIDR .LoUU == 0b000 ).
Data cache clean to the Point of Unification is not required for instruction to data coherence.

fields.name: CWG
fields.description: Cache writeback granule. Log 2 of the number of words of the maximum size of memory that can be overwritten as a result of the eviction of a cache entry that has had a memory location in it modified. A value of 0b0000 indicates that this register does not provide Cache writeback granule information and either: The architectural maximum of 512 words (2KB) must be assumed. The Cache writeback granule can be determined from maximum cache line size encoded in the Cache Size ID Registers. Values greater than 0b1001 are reserved. Arm recommends that an implementation that does not support cache write-back implements this field as 0b0001 . This applies, for example, to an implementation that supports only write-through caches.

fields.name: ERG
fields.description: Exclusives reservation granule. Log 2 of the number of words of the maximum size of the reservation granule that has been implemented for the Load-Exclusive and Store-Exclusive instructions. The use of the value 0b0000 is deprecated. The value 0b0001 and values greater than 0b1001 are reserved.

fields.name: DminLine
fields.description: Log 2 of the number of words in the smallest cache line of all the data caches and unified caches that are controlled by the PE.

fields.name: L1Ip
fields.description: Level 1 instruction cache policy. Indicates the indexing and tagging policy for the L1 instruction cache.

values.label: Reserved.
ASID-tagged Virtual Index, Virtual Tag (AIVIVT).
Virtual Index, Physical Tag (VIPT).
Physical Index, Physical Tag (PIPT).

fields.name: RES0
fields.description: Reserved, RES0 .

fields.name: IminLine
fields.description: Log 2 of the number of words in the smallest cache line of all the instruction caches that are controlled by the PE.

name: DBGBCR<n>
description:
Holds control information for a breakpoint.
Forms breakpoint n together with value register DBGBVR<n> .
If EL2 is implemented and this breakpoint supports Context matching, DBGBVR<n> can be associated with a Breakpoint Extended Value
Register DBGBXVR<n> for VMID matching.

fields.name: RES0
fields.description: Reserved, RES0 .

fields.name: BT
fields.description: Breakpoint Type. Possible values are:

values.label: Unlinked instruction address match. DBGBVR<n> is the address of an instruction.
As 0b0000 , but linked to a Context matching breakpoint.

Unlinked Context ID match. If the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .E2H is 1, and either the PE is executing at EL0 with HCR_EL2 .TGE set to 1 or the PE is executing at EL2, then DBGBVR<n> .ContextID is compared against CONTEXTIDR_EL2 . Otherwise, DBGBVR<n> .ContextID is compared against CONTEXTIDR .
As 0b0010 with linking enabled.
Unlinked instruction address mismatch. DBGBVR<n> is the address of an instruction to be stepped.
As 0b0100 , but linked to a Context matching breakpoint.
Unlinked CONTEXTIDR match. DBGBVR<n> .ContextID is a Context ID compared against CONTEXTIDR .
As 0b0110 with linking enabled.
Unlinked VMID match. DBGBXVR<n> .VMID is a VMID compared against VTTBR .VMID.
As 0b1000 with linking enabled.
Unlinked VMID and Context ID match. DBGBVR<n> .ContextID is a Context ID compared against CONTEXTIDR , and DBGBXVR<n> .VMID is a VMID compared against VTTBR .VMID.
As 0b1010 with linking enabled.
Unlinked CONTEXTIDR_EL2 match. DBGBXVR<n> .ContextID2 is a Context ID compared against CONTEXTIDR_EL2 .
As 0b1100 with linking enabled.
Unlinked Full Context ID match. DBGBVR<n> .ContextID is compared against CONTEXTIDR , and DBGBXVR<n> .ContextID2 is compared against CONTEXTIDR_EL2 .
As 0b1110 with linking enabled.

fields.name: LBN
fields.description: Linked Breakpoint Number. For Linked address matching breakpoints, this specifies the index of the Context-matching breakpoint linked to. For all other breakpoint types, this field is ignored and reads of the register return an UNKNOWN value. This field is ignored when the value of DBGBCR<n>.E is 0.

fields.name: SSC
fields.description: Security state control. Determines the Security states under which a Breakpoint debug event for breakpoint n is generated. This field must be interpreted along with the HMC and PMC fields, and there are constraints on the permitted values of the {HMC, SSC, PMC} fields. For more information, see 'Execution conditions for which a breakpoint generates Breakpoint exceptions' and 'Reserved DBGBCR<n>.{SSC, HMC, PMC} values' .

fields.name: HMC
fields.description: Higher mode control. Determines the debug perspective for deciding when a Breakpoint debug event for breakpoint n is generated. This field must be interpreted along with the SSC and PMC fields, and there are constraints on the permitted values of the {HMC, SSC, PMC} fields. For more information see the SSC, bits [15:14] description. For more information on the operation of the SSC, HMC, and PMC fields, see 'Execution conditions for which a breakpoint generates Breakpoint exceptions' .

fields.name: RES0
fields.description: Reserved, RES0 .

fields.name: BAS
fields.description: Byte address select. Defines which half-words an address-matching breakpoint matches, regardless of the instruction set and Execution state. The permitted values depend on the breakpoint type. For Address match breakpoints, the permitted values are: BAS Match instruction at Constraint for debuggers 0b0011 DBGBVR<n> Use for T32 instructions 0b1100 DBGBVR<n> +2 Use for T32 instructions 0b1111 DBGBVR<n> Use for A32 instructions All other values are reserved. For more information, see 'Reserved DBGBCR<n>.BAS values' . For more information on using the BAS field in Address Match breakpoints, see 'Using the BAS field in Address Match breakpoints' . For Address mismatch breakpoints in an AArch32 stage 1 translation regime, the permitted values are: BAS Step instruction at Constraint for debuggers 0b0000 - Use for a match anywhere breakpoint 0b0011 DBGBVR<n> Use for T32 instructions 0b1100 DBGBVR<n> +2 Use for T32 instructions 0b1111 DBGBVR<n> Use for A32 instructions All other values are reserved. For more information, see 'Reserved DBGBCR<n>.BAS values' . For more information on using the BAS field in address mismatch breakpoints, see 'Using the BAS field in Address Match breakpoints' . For Context matching breakpoints, this field is RES1 and ignored.

fields.name: RES0
fields.description: Reserved, RES0 .

fields.name: PMC
fields.description: Privilege mode control. Determines the Exception level or levels at which a Breakpoint debug event for breakpoint n is generated. This field must be interpreted along with the SSC and HMC fields, and there are constraints on the permitted values of the {HMC, SSC, PMC} fields. For more information see the DBGBCR<n>.SSC description. For more information on the operation of the SSC, HMC, and PMC fields, see 'Execution conditions for which a breakpoint generates Breakpoint exceptions' .

fields.name: E

fields.description: Byte address select. Each bit of this field selects whether a byte from within the word or double-word addressed by DBGWVR<n> is being watched. BAS Description 0bxxxxxxx1 Match byte at DBGWVR<n> 0bxxxxxx1x Match byte at DBGWVR<n> +1 0bxxxxx1xx Match byte at DBGWVR<n> +2 0bxxxx1xxx Match byte at DBGWVR<n> +3 In cases where DBGWVR<n> addresses a double-word: BAS Description, if DBGWVR<n> [2] == 0 0bxxx1xxxx Match byte at DBGWVR<n> +4 0bxx1xxxxx Match byte at DBGWVR<n> +5 0bx1xxxxxx Match byte at DBGWVR<n> +6 0b1xxxxxxx Match byte at DBGWVR<n> +7 If DBGWVR<n> [2] == 1, only BAS[3:0] are used and BAS[7:4] are ignored. This control permits access to the Advanced SIMD and floating-point functionality at all Exception levels.

fields.name: DEX
fields.description: Defined synchronous exception on floating-point execution. This field identifies whether a synchronous exception generated by the attempted execution of an instruction was generated by an unallocated encoding. The instruction must be in the encoding space that is identified by the pseudocode function ExecutingCP10or11Instr() returning TRUE. This field also indicates whether the FPEXC.TFV field is valid. The meaning of this bit is:

values.label: The exception was generated by the attempted execution of an unallocated instruction in the encoding space that is identified by the pseudocode function ExecutingCP10or11Instr(). If FPEXC.TFV is RW then it is invalid and UNKNOWN . If FPEXC.{IDF, IXF, UFF, OFF, DZF, IOF} are RW then they are invalid and UNKNOWN .
The exception was generated during the execution of an allocated encoding. FPEXC.TFV is valid and indicates the cause of the exception.

fields.name: TFV
fields.description: Trapped Fault Valid bit. Valid only when the value of FPEXC.DEX is 1. When valid, it indicates the cause of the exception and therefore whether FPEXC.{IDF, IXF, UFF, OFF, DZF, IOF} are valid.

values.label: The exception was caused by the execution of a floating-point VABS, VADD, VDIV, VFMA, VFMS, VFNMA, VFNMS, VMLA, VMLS, VMOV, VMUL, VNEG, VNMLA, VNMLS, VNMUL, VSQRT, or VSUB instruction when one or both of FPSCR .{Stride, Len} was nonzero. If FPEXC.{IDF, IXF, UFF, OFF, DZF, IOF} are RW then they are invalid and UNKNOWN .

FPEXC.{IDF, IXF, UFF, OFF, DZF, IOF} indicate the presence of trapped floating-point exceptions that had occurred at the time of the exception. Bits are set for all trapped exceptions that had occurred at the time of the exception.

fields.name: RES0

name: HMAIR0
description: Along with HMAIR1 , provides the memory attribute encodings corresponding to the possible AttrIndx values in a Long-descriptor format translation table entry for stage 1 translations for memory accesses from Hyp mode. AttrIndx[2] indicates the HMAIR register to be used: When AttrIndx[2] is 0, HMAIR0 is used. When AttrIndx[2] is 1, HMAIR1 is used.

fields.name: Attr<n>
fields.description: The memory attribute encoding for an AttrIndx[2:0] entry in a Long descriptor format translation table entry, where: AttrIndx[2:0] gives the value of <n> in Attr<n>. AttrIndx[2] defines which MAIR to access. Attr7 to Attr4 are in MAIR1, and Attr3 to Attr0 are in MAIR0. Bits [7:4] are encoded as follows: Attr<n>[7:4] Meaning 0b0000 Device memory. See encoding of Attr<n>[3:0] for the type of Device memory. 0b00RW, RW not 0b00 Normal memory, Outer Write-Through Transient. 0b0100 Normal memory, Outer Non-cacheable. 0b01RW, RW not 0b00 Normal memory, Outer Write-Back Transient. 0b10RW Normal memory, Outer Write-Through Non-transient. 0b11RW Normal memory, Outer Write-Back Non-transient. R = Outer Read-Allocate policy, W = Outer Write-Allocate policy. The meaning of bits [3:0] depends on the value of bits [7:4]: Attr<n>[3:0] Meaning when Attr<n>[7:4] is 0b0000 Meaning when Attr<n>[7:4] is not 0b0000 0b0000 Device-nGnRnE memory UNPREDICTABLE 0b00RW, RW not 0b00 UNPREDICTABLE Normal memory, Inner Write-Through Transient 0b0100 Device-nGnRE memory Normal memory, Inner Non-cacheable 0b01RW, RW not 0b00 UNPREDICTABLE Normal memory, Inner Write-Back Transient 0b1000 Device-nGRE memory Normal memory, Inner Write-Through Non-transient (RW= 0b00 ) 0b10RW, RW not 0b00 UNPREDICTABLE Normal memory, Inner Write-Through Non-transient 0b1100 Device-GRE memory Normal memory, Inner Write-Back Non-transient (RW= 0b00 ) 0b11RW, RW not 0b00 UNPREDICTABLE Normal memory, Inner Write-Back Non-transient R = Inner Read-Allocate policy, W = Inner Write-Allocate policy. The R and W bits in some Attr<n> fields have the following meanings: R or W Meaning 0b0 No Allocate 0b1 Allocate When FEAT_XS is implemented, stage 1 Inner Write-Back Cacheable, Outer Write-Back Cacheable memory types have the XS attribute set to 0.

R = Outer Read-Allocate policy, W = Outer Write-Allocate policy. Performance Monitors Extension, PMUv1 implemented.

PMUv3 for Armv8.1. As 0b0011 , and adds support for: Extended 16-bit PMEVTYPER<n> .evtCount field. If EL2 is implemented, the HDCR .HPMD control.
PMUv3 for Armv8.4. As 0b0100 , and adds support for the PMMIR register.

PMUv3 for Armv8.8. As 0b0111 , and: Extends the Common event number space to include 0x0040 to 0x00BF and 0x4040 to 0x40BF . Removes the CONSTRAINED UNPREDICTABLE behaviors if a reserved or unimplemented PMU event number is selected.
PMUv3 for Armv8.9. As 0b1000 , and: Updates the definitions of existing PMU events. Adds support for the EDECR .PME control.

values.label: Not implemented. This indicates that the User bank and Exception return forms of the LDM and STM instructions are not implemented.
Adds the LDM (exception return), LDM (user registers), and STM (user registers) instruction versions.

fields.name: Endian
fields.description: Indicates the implemented Endian instructions.

values.label: None implemented.
Adds the SETEND instruction, and the E bit in the PSRs.

name: ID_ISAR2
description: Provides information about the instruction sets implemented by the PE in AArch32 state. Must be interpreted with ID_ISAR0 , ID_ISAR1 , ID_ISAR3 , ID_ISAR4 , and ID_ISAR5 . For general information about the interpretation of the ID registers see 'Principles of the ID scheme for fields in ID registers' .

fields.name: Reversal
fields.description: Indicates the implemented Reversal instructions.

values.label: None implemented.
Adds the REV , REV16 , and REVSH instructions.
As for 0b0001 , and adds the RBIT instruction.

fields.name: PSR_AR
fields.description: Indicates the implemented A and R-profile instructions to manipulate the PSR.

values.label: None implemented.
Adds the MRS and MSR instructions, and the exception return forms of data-processing instructions.

fields.name: MultU
fields.description: Indicates the implemented advanced unsigned Multiply instructions.

values.label: None implemented.
Adds the UMULL and UMLAL instructions.
As for 0b0001 , and adds the UMAAL instruction.

fields.name: MultS
fields.description: Indicates the implemented advanced signed Multiply instructions.

values.label: None implemented.
Adds the SMULL and SMLAL instructions.

As for 0b0001 , and adds the SMLABB , SMLABT , SMLALBB , SMLALBT , SMLALTB , SMLALTT , SMLATB , SMLATT , SMLAWB , SMLAWT , SMULBB , SMULBT , SMULTB , SMULTT , SMULWB , and SMULWT instructions. Also adds the Q bit in the PSRs.

As for 0b0010 , and adds the SMLAD , SMLADX , SMLALD , SMLALDX , SMLSD , SMLSDX , SMLSLD , SMLSLDX , SMMLA , SMMLAR , SMMLS , SMMLSR , SMMUL , SMMULR , SMUAD , SMUADX , SMUSD , and SMUSDX instructions.

fields.name: Mult
fields.description: Indicates the implemented additional Multiply instructions.

values.label: No additional instructions implemented. This means only MUL is implemented.
Adds the MLA instruction.
As for 0b0001 , and adds the MLS instruction.

fields.name: MultiAccessInt
fields.description: Indicates the support for interruptible multi-access instructions.

values.label: No support. This means the LDM and STM instructions are not interruptible.
LDM and STM instructions are restartable.
LDM and STM instructions are continuable.

fields.name: MemHint
fields.description: Indicates the implemented Memory Hint instructions.

values.label: None implemented.
Adds the PLD instruction.
Adds the PLD instruction. ( 0b0001 and 0b0010 have identical effects.)
As for 0b0001 (or 0b0010 ), and adds the PLI instruction.
As for 0b0011 , and adds the PLDW instruction.

fields.name: LoadStore
fields.description: Indicates the implemented additional load/store instructions.

values.label: No additional load/store instructions implemented.
Adds the LDRD and STRD instructions.
As for 0b0001 , and adds the Load Acquire ( LDAB , LDAH , LDA , LDAEXB , LDAEXH , LDAEX , LDAEXD ) and Store Release ( STLB , STLH , STL , STLEXB , STLEXH , STLEX , STLEXD ) instructions.

name: ID_ISAR3
description: Provides information about the instruction sets implemented by the PE in AArch32 state. Must be interpreted with ID_ISAR0 , ID_ISAR1 , ID_ISAR2 , ID_ISAR4 , and ID_ISAR5 . For general information about the interpretation of the ID registers see 'Principles of the ID scheme for fields in ID registers' .

fields.name: T32EE
fields.description: Indicates the implemented T32EE instructions.

values.label: None implemented.
Adds the ENTERX and LEAVEX instructions, and modifies the load behavior to include null checking.

fields.name: TrueNOP
fields.description: Indicates the implemented true NOP instructions.

values.label: None implemented. This means there are no NOP instructions that do not have any register dependencies.
Adds true NOP instructions in both the T32 and A32 instruction sets. This also permits additional NOP-compatible hints.

fields.name: T32Copy
fields.description: Indicates the support for T32 non flag-setting MOV instructions.

values.label: Not supported. This means that in the T32 instruction set, encoding T1 of the MOV (register) instruction does not support a copy from a low register to a low register.
Adds support for T32 instruction set encoding T1 of the MOV (register) instruction, copying from a low register to a low register.

fields.name: TabBranch
fields.description: Indicates the implemented Table Branch instructions in the T32 instruction set.

values.label: None implemented.
Adds the TBB and TBH instructions.

fields.name: SynchPrim
fields.description: Used in conjunction with ID_ISAR4.SynchPrim_frac to indicate the implemented Synchronization Primitive instructions.

values.label: If SynchPrim_frac == 0b000 , no Synchronization Primitives implemented.
If SynchPrim_frac == 0b000 , adds the LDREX and STREX instructions. If SynchPrim_frac == 0b011 , also adds the CLREX , LDREXB , STREXB , and STREXH instructions.
If SynchPrim_frac == 0b000 , as for [ 0b001 , 0b011 ] and also adds the LDREXD and STREXD instructions.

fields.name: SVC
fields.description: Indicates the implemented SVC instructions.

values.label: Not implemented.
Adds the SVC instruction.

fields.name: SIMD
fields.description: Indicates the implemented SIMD instructions.

values.label: None implemented.
Adds the SSAT and USAT instructions, and the Q bit in the PSRs.

As for 0b0001 , and adds the PKHBT , PKHTB , QADD16 , QADD8 , QASX , QSUB16 , QSUB8 , QSAX , SADD16 , SADD8 , SASX , SEL , SHADD16 , SHADD8 , SHASX , SHSUB16 , SHSUB8 , SHSAX , SSAT16 , SSUB16 , SSUB8 , SSAX , SXTAB16 , SXTB16 , UADD16 , UADD8 , UASX , UHADD16 , UHADD8 , UHASX , UHSUB16 , UHSUB8 , UHSAX , UQADD16 , UQADD8 , UQASX , UQSUB16 , UQSUB8 , UQSAX , USAD8 , USADA8 , USAT16 , USUB16 , USUB8 , USAX , UXTAB16 , and UXTB16 instructions. Also adds support for the GE[3:0] bits in the PSRs.

fields.name: Saturate
fields.description: Indicates support for Saturate instructions QADD , QDADD , QDSUB , and QSUB instructions, and the Q bit in the PSRs.

values.label: None implemented. This means no non-Advanced SIMD saturate instructions are implemented.
The specified instructions are implemented.

name: ID_ISAR4
description: Provides information about the instruction sets implemented by the PE in AArch32 state. Must be interpreted with ID_ISAR0 , ID_ISAR1 , ID_ISAR2 , ID_ISAR3 , and ID_ISAR5 . For general information about the interpretation of the ID registers, see 'Principles of the ID scheme for fields in ID registers' .

fields.name: SWP_frac
fields.description: Indicates support for the memory system locking the bus for SWP or SWPB instructions.

values.label: SWP or SWPB instructions not implemented.

SWP or SWPB implemented but only in a uniprocessor context. SWP and SWPB do not guarantee whether memory accesses from other Requesters can come between the load memory access and the store memory access of the SWP or SWPB .

fields.name: PSR_M
fields.description: Indicates the implemented M-profile instructions to modify the PSRs.

values.label: None implemented.
Adds the M-profile forms of the CPS , MRS , and MSR instructions.

fields.name: SynchPrim_frac
fields.description: Used in conjunction with ID_ISAR3 .SynchPrim to indicate the implemented Synchronization Primitive instructions.

values.label: If SynchPrim == 0b0000 , no Synchronization Primitives implemented. If SynchPrim == 0b0001 , adds the LDREX and STREX instructions. If SynchPrim == 0b0010 , also adds the CLREX , LDREXB , LDREXH , STREXB , STREXH , LDREXD , and STREXD instructions.
If SynchPrim == 0b0001 , adds the LDREX , STREX , CLREX , LDREXB , LDREXH , STREXB , and STREXH instructions.

fields.name: Barrier
fields.description: Indicates support for Barrier instructions DMB , DSB , and ISB in the T32 and A32 instruction sets.

values.label: None implemented. Barrier operations are provided only as System instructions in the (coproc== 0b1111 ) encoding space.
The specified instructions are implemented.

fields.name: SMC
fields.description: Indicates the implemented SMC instructions.

values.label: None implemented.
Adds the SMC instruction.

fields.name: Writeback
fields.description: Indicates the support for Writeback addressing modes.

values.label: Basic support. Only the LDM , STM , PUSH , POP , SRS , and RFE instructions support writeback addressing modes. These instructions support all of their writeback addressing modes.
Adds support for all of the writeback addressing modes.

fields.name: WithShifts
fields.description: Indicates the support for instructions with shifts.

values.label: Nonzero shifts supported only in MOV and shift instructions.
Adds support for shifts of loads and stores over the range LSL 0-3.
As for 0b0001 , and adds support for other constant shift options, both on load/store and other instructions.
As for 0b0011 , and adds support for register-controlled shift options.

fields.name: Unpriv
fields.description: Indicates the implemented unprivileged instructions.

values.label: None implemented. No T variant instructions are implemented.
Adds the LDRBT , LDRT , STRBT , and STRT instructions.
As for 0b0001 , and adds the LDRHT , LDRSBT , LDRSHT , and STRHT instructions.

name: ID_ISAR5
description: Provides information about the instruction sets implemented by the PE in AArch32 state. Must be interpreted with ID_ISAR0 , ID_ISAR1 , ID_ISAR2 , ID_ISAR3 , and ID_ISAR4 . For general information about the interpretation of the ID registers see 'Principles of the ID scheme for fields in ID registers' .

fields.name: VCMA
fields.description: Indicates AArch32 support for complex number addition and multiplication where numbers are stored in vectors.

values.label: The VCMLA and VCADD instructions are not implemented in AArch32.
The VCMLA and VCADD instructions are implemented in AArch32.

fields.name: RDM
fields.description: Indicates support for the VQRDMLAH and VQRDMLSH instructions in AArch32 state.

values.label: No VQRDMLAH and VQRDMLSH instructions implemented.
VQRDMLAH and VQRDMLSH instructions implemented.

fields.name: RES0
fields.description: Reserved, RES0 .

fields.name: CRC32
fields.description: Indicates support for the CRC32 instructions CRC32B , CRC32H , CRC32W , CRC32CB , CRC32CH , and CRC32CW in AArch32 state.

values.label: CRC32 instructions are not implemented.
The specified instructions are implemented.

fields.name: SHA2
fields.description: Indicates support for the SHA2 instructions SHA256H , SHA256H2 , SHA256SU0 , and SHA256SU1 in AArch32 state.

values.label: No SHA2 instructions implemented.
The specified instructions are implemented.

fields.name: SHA1
fields.description: Indicates support for the SHA1 instructions SHA1C , SHA1P , SHA1M , SHA1H , SHA1SU0 , and SHA1SU1 in AArch32 state.

values.label: No SHA1 instructions implemented.
The specified instructions are implemented.

fields.name: AES
fields.description: Indicates support for the AES instructions in AArch32 state.

values.label: No AES instructions implemented.
AESE , AESD , AESMC , and AESIMC implemented.
As for 0b0001 , plus VMULL (polynomial) instructions operating on 64-bit data quantities.

fields.name: SEVL
fields.description: Indicates support for the SEVL instruction in AArch32 state.

values.label: SEVL is implemented as a NOP.
SEVL is implemented as Send Event Local.

name: ID_MMFR1
description: Provides information about the implemented memory model and memory management support in AArch32 state. For general information about the interpretation of the ID registers see 'Principles of the ID scheme for fields in ID registers' .

fields.name: BPred
fields.description: Branch Predictor. Indicates branch predictor management requirements.

values.label: No branch predictor, or no MMU present. Implies a fixed MPU configuration.

Branch predictor requires flushing on: Enabling or disabling a stage of address translation. Writing new data to instruction locations. Writing new mappings to the translation tables. Changes to the TTBR0 , TTBR1 , or TTBCR registers. Changes to the ContextID or ASID, or to the FCSE ProcessID if this is supported.

Branch predictor requires flushing on: Enabling or disabling a stage of address translation. Writing new data to instruction locations. Writing new mappings to the translation tables. Any change to the TTBR0 , TTBR1 , or TTBCR registers without a change to the corresponding ContextID or ASID, or FCSE ProcessID if this is supported.
Branch predictor requires flushing only on writing new data to instruction locations.
For execution correctness, branch predictor requires no flushing at any time.

fields.name: L1TstCln
fields.description: Level 1 cache Test and Clean. Indicates the supported Level 1 data cache test and clean operations, for Harvard or unified cache implementations.

values.label: None supported.
Supported Level 1 data cache test and clean operations are: Test and clean data cache.
As for 0b0001 , and adds: Test, clean, and invalidate data cache.

fields.name: L1Uni
fields.description: Level 1 Unified cache. Indicates the supported entire Level 1 cache maintenance operations for a unified cache implementation.

values.label: None supported.
Supported entire Level 1 cache operations are: Invalidate cache, including branch predictor if appropriate. Invalidate branch predictor, if appropriate.
As for 0b0001 , and adds: Clean cache, using a recursive model that uses the cache dirty status bit. Clean and invalidate cache, using a recursive model that uses the cache dirty status bit.

fields.name: L1Hvd
fields.description: Level 1 Harvard cache. Indicates the supported entire Level 1 cache maintenance operations for a Harvard cache implementation.

values.label: None supported.
Supported entire Level 1 cache operations are: Invalidate instruction cache, including branch predictor if appropriate. Invalidate branch predictor, if appropriate.
As for 0b0001 , and adds: Invalidate data cache. Invalidate data cache and instruction cache, including branch predictor if appropriate.

As for 0b0010 , and adds: Clean data cache, using a recursive model that uses the cache dirty status bit. Clean and invalidate data cache, using a recursive model that uses the cache dirty status bit.

fields.name: L1UniSW
fields.description: Level 1 Unified cache by Set/Way. Indicates the supported Level 1 cache line maintenance operations by set/way, for a unified cache implementation.

values.label: None supported.
Supported Level 1 unified cache line maintenance operations by set/way are: Clean cache line by set/way.
As for 0b0001 , and adds: Clean and invalidate cache line by set/way.
As for 0b0010 , and adds: Invalidate cache line by set/way.

fields.name: L1HvdSW
fields.description: Level 1 Harvard cache by Set/Way. Indicates the supported Level 1 cache line maintenance operations by set/way, for a Harvard cache implementation.

values.label: None supported.
Supported Level 1 Harvard cache line maintenance operations by set/way are: Clean data cache line by set/way. Clean and invalidate data cache line by set/way.
As for 0b0001 , and adds: Invalidate data cache line by set/way.
As for 0b0010 , and adds: Invalidate instruction cache line by set/way.

fields.name: L1UniVA
fields.description: Level 1 Unified cache by Virtual Address. Indicates the supported Level 1 cache line maintenance operations by VA, for a unified cache implementation.

values.label: None supported.
Supported Level 1 unified cache line maintenance operations by VA are: Clean cache line by VA. Invalidate cache line by VA. Clean and invalidate cache line by VA.
As for 0b0001 , and adds: Invalidate branch predictor by VA, if branch predictor is implemented.

fields.name: L1HvdVA
fields.description: Level 1 Harvard cache by Virtual Address. Indicates the supported Level 1 cache line maintenance operations by VA, for a Harvard cache implementation.

values.label: None supported.

Supported Level 1 Harvard cache line maintenance operations by VA are: Clean data cache line by VA. Invalidate data cache line by VA. Clean and invalidate data cache line by VA. Clean instruction cache line by VA.
As for 0b0001 , and adds: Invalidate branch predictor by VA, if branch predictor is implemented.

name: ID_MMFR3
description: Provides information about the implemented memory model and memory management support in AArch32 state. For general information about the interpretation of the ID registers see 'Principles of the ID scheme for fields in ID registers' .

fields.name: Supersec
fields.description: Supersections. On a VMSA implementation, indicates whether Supersections are supported.

values.label: Supersections supported.
Supersections not supported.

fields.name: CMemSz
fields.description: Cached Memory Size. Indicates the physical memory size supported by the caches.

values.label: 4GB, corresponding to a 32-bit physical address range.
64GB, corresponding to a 36-bit physical address range.
1TB or more, corresponding to a 40-bit or larger physical address range.

fields.name: CohWalk
fields.description: Coherent Walk. Indicates whether Translation table updates require a clean to the Point of Unification.

values.label: Updates to the translation tables require a clean to the Point of Unification to ensure visibility by subsequent translation table walks.
Updates to the translation tables do not require a clean to the Point of Unification to ensure visibility by subsequent translation table walks.

fields.name: PAN
fields.description: Privileged Access Never. Indicates support for the PAN bit in CPSR , SPSR , and DSPSR in AArch32 state.

values.label: PAN not supported.
PAN supported.
PAN supported and ATS1CPRP and ATS1CPWP instructions supported.

fields.name: MaintBcst
fields.description: Maintenance Broadcast. Indicates whether Cache, TLB, and branch predictor operations are broadcast.

values.label: Cache, TLB, and branch predictor operations only affect local structures.
Cache and branch predictor operations affect structures according to shareability and defined behavior of instructions. TLB operations only affect local structures.
Cache, TLB, and branch predictor operations affect structures according to shareability and defined behavior of instructions.

fields.name: BPMaint
fields.description: Branch Predictor Maintenance. Indicates the supported branch predictor maintenance operations in an implementation with hierarchical cache maintenance operations.

values.label: None supported.
Supported branch predictor maintenance operations are: Invalidate all branch predictors.
As for 0b0001 , and adds: Invalidate branch predictors by VA.

fields.name: CMaintSW
fields.description: Cache Maintenance by Set/Way. Indicates the supported cache maintenance operations by set/way, in an implementation with hierarchical caches.

values.label: None supported.
Supported hierarchical cache maintenance instructions by set/way are: Invalidate data cache by set/way. Clean data cache by set/way. Clean and invalidate data cache by set/way.

fields.name: CMaintVA
fields.description: Cache Maintenance by Virtual Address. Indicates the supported cache maintenance operations by VA, in an implementation with hierarchical caches.

values.label: None supported.

Supported hierarchical cache maintenance operations by VA are: Invalidate data cache by VA. Clean data cache by VA. Clean and invalidate data cache by VA. Invalidate instruction cache by VA. Invalidate all instruction cache entries.

name: ID_MMFR4
description: Provides information about the implemented memory model and memory management support in AArch32 state. For general information about the interpretation of the ID registers see 'Principles of the ID scheme for fields in ID registers' .

fields.name: EVT
fields.description: Enhanced Virtualization Traps. If EL2 is implemented, indicates support for the HCR2 .{TTLBIS, TOCU, TICAB, TID4} traps.

values.label: HCR2 .{TTLBIS, TOCU, TICAB, TID4} traps are not supported.
HCR2 .{TOCU, TICAB, TID4} traps are supported.
As 0b0001 , and HCR2 .TTLBIS trap is supported.

fields.name: CCIDX
fields.description: Support for use of the revised CCSIDR format and the presence of the CCSIDR2 is indicated.

values.label: 32-bit format implemented for all levels of the CCSIDR, and the CCSIDR2 register is not implemented.
64-bit format implemented for all levels of the CCSIDR, and the CCSIDR2 register is implemented.

fields.name: LSM
fields.description: Indicates support for LSMAOE and nTLSMD bits in HSCTLR and SCTLR .

values.label: LSMAOE and nTLSMD bits not supported.
LSMAOE and nTLSMD bits supported.

fields.name: HPDS
fields.description: Hierarchical permission disables bits in translation tables.

values.label: Disabling of hierarchical controls not supported.
Supports disabling of hierarchical controls using the TTBCR2 .HPD0, TTBCR2 .HPD1, and HTCR .HPD bits.
As for value 0b0001 , and adds possible hardware allocation of bits[62:59] of the Translation table descriptors from the final lookup level for IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED use.

fields.name: CnP
fields.description: Common not Private translations.

values.label: Common not Private translations not supported.
Common not Private translations supported.

fields.name: XNX
fields.description: Support for execute-never control distinction by Exception level at stage 2.

values.label: Distinction between EL0 and EL1 execute-never control at stage 2 not supported.
Distinction between EL0 and EL1 execute-never control at stage 2 supported.

fields.name: AC2
fields.description: Indicates the extension of the ACTLR and HACTLR registers using ACTLR2 and HACTLR2 .

values.label: ACTLR2 and HACTLR2 are not implemented.
ACTLR2 and HACTLR2 are implemented.

fields.name: SpecSEI
fields.description: Describes whether the PE can generate SError exceptions from speculative reads of memory, including speculative instruction fetches.

values.label: The PE never generates an SError exception due to an External abort on a speculative read.
The PE might generate an SError exception due to an External abort on a speculative read.

fields.name: RES0
fields.description: Reserved, RES0 .

name: ID_PFR0
description: Gives top-level information about the instruction sets and other features supported by the PE in AArch32 state. Must be interpreted with ID_PFR1 . For general information about the interpretation of the ID registers, see 'Principles of the ID scheme for fields in ID registers' .

fields.name: RAS
fields.description: RAS Extension version.

values.label: The RAS Extension is not implemented.
The RAS Extension is implemented, FEAT_RAS provides the ESB instruction and the Error synchronization event.

FEAT_RASv1p1 implemented. As 0b0001 , and adds support for additional ERXMISC<m> System registers. Error records accessed through System registers conform to RAS System Architecture v1.1, which includes simplifications to ERR<n>STATUS and support for the optional RAS Timestamp Extension.
FEAT_RASv2 implemented. As 0b0010 , and requires that error records accessed through System registers conform to either RAS System Architecture v1.1 or RAS System Architecture v2.

fields.name: DIT
fields.description: Data Independent Timing.

values.label: AArch32 does not guarantee constant execution time of any instructions.
AArch32 provides the PSTATE.DIT mechanism to guarantee constant execution time of certain instructions.

fields.name: AMU
fields.description: Indicates support for Activity Monitors Extension.

values.label: Activity Monitors Extension is not implemented.
FEAT_AMUv1 is implemented.
FEAT_AMUv1p1 is implemented. As 0b0001 and adds support for virtualization of the activity monitor event counters.

fields.name: CSV2
fields.description: Speculative use of out of context branch targets.

values.label: The implementation does not disclose whether FEAT_CSV2 is implemented.
FEAT_CSV2 is implemented, but FEAT_CSV2_1p1 is not implemented.
FEAT_CSV2_1p1 is implemented.

fields.name: State3
fields.description: T32EE instruction set support.

values.label: Not implemented.
T32EE instruction set implemented.

fields.name: State2
fields.description: Jazelle extension support.

values.label: Not implemented.
Jazelle extension implemented, without clearing of JOSCR .CV on exception entry.
Jazelle extension implemented, with clearing of JOSCR .CV on exception entry.

fields.name: State1
fields.description: T32 instruction set support.

values.label: T32 instruction set not implemented.

T32 encodings before the introduction of Thumb-2 technology implemented: All instructions are 16-bit. A BL or BLX is a pair of 16-bit instructions. 32-bit instructions other than BL and BLX cannot be encoded.
T32 encodings after the introduction of Thumb-2 technology implemented, for all 16-bit and 32-bit T32 basic instructions.

fields.name: State0
fields.description: A32 instruction set support.

values.label: A32 instruction set not implemented.
A32 instruction set implemented.

name: ID_PFR1
description: Gives information about the AArch32 programmers' model. Must be interpreted with ID_PFR0 . For general information about the interpretation of the ID registers see 'Principles of the ID scheme for fields in ID registers' .

fields.name: GIC
fields.description: System register GIC CPU interface.

values.label: GIC CPU interface system registers not implemented.
System register interface to versions 3.0 and 4.0 of the GIC CPU interface is supported.
System register interface to version 4.1 of the GIC CPU interface is supported.

fields.name: Virt_frac
fields.description: Virtualization fractional field. When the Virtualization field is 0b0000 , determines the support for Virtualization Extensions.

values.label: No Virtualization Extensions are implemented.

The following Virtualization Extensions are implemented: The SCR .SIF bit, if EL3 is implemented. The modifications to the SCR .AW and SCR .FW bits described in the Virtualization Extensions, if EL3 is implemented. The MSR (banked register) and MRS (banked register) instructions. The ERET instruction.

fields.name: Sec_frac
fields.description: Security fractional field. When the Security field is 0b0000 , determines the support for Security Extensions.

values.label: No Security Extensions are implemented.
The following Security Extensions are implemented: The VBAR register. The TTBCR .PD0 and TTBCR .PD1 bits.
As for 0b0001 , and the ability to access Secure or Non-secure physical memory is supported.

fields.name: GenTimer
fields.description: Generic Timer support.

values.label: Generic Timer is not implemented.
Generic Timer is implemented.
Generic Timer is implemented, and also includes support for CNTHCTL .EVNTIS and CNTKCTL .EVNTIS fields, and CNTPCTSS and CNTVCTSS counter views.

fields.name: Virtualization
fields.description: Virtualization support.

values.label: EL2, Hyp mode, and the HVC instruction not implemented.
EL2, Hyp mode, the HVC instruction, and all the features described by Virt_frac == 0b0001 implemented.

fields.name: MProgMod
fields.description: M-profile programmers' model support.

values.label: Not supported.
Support for two-stack programmers' model.

fields.name: Security
fields.description: Security support.

values.label: EL3, Monitor mode, and the SMC instruction not implemented.
EL3, Monitor mode, the SMC instruction, and all the features described by Sec_frac == 0b0001 implemented.
As for 0b0001 , and adds the ability to set the NSACR .RFR bit. Not permitted in Armv8 as the NSACR .RFR bit is RES0 .

fields.name: ProgMod
fields.description: Support for the standard programmers' model for ARMv4 and later. Model must support User, FIQ, IRQ, Supervisor, Abort, Undefined, and System modes.

values.label: Not supported.
Supported.

name: IFAR
description: Holds the virtual address of the faulting address that caused a synchronous Prefetch Abort exception.

fields.name: VA
fields.description: VA of faulting address of synchronous Prefetch Abort exception.

name: ISR
description: Shows the pending status of the IRQ and FIQ interrupts and the SError exceptions.

fields.name: RES0
fields.description: Reserved, RES0 .

fields.name: A
fields.description: SError exception pending bit:

values.label: No pending SError exception.
An SError exception is pending.

fields.name: I
fields.description: IRQ pending bit. Indicates whether an IRQ interrupt is pending:

values.label: No pending IRQ.
An IRQ interrupt is pending.

fields.name: F
fields.description: FIQ pending bit. Indicates whether an FIQ interrupt is pending.

values.label: No pending FIQ.
An FIQ interrupt is pending.

fields.name: RES0
fields.description: Reserved, RES0 .

name: JIDR
description: A Jazelle register, which identified the Jazelle architecture version.

fields.name: RAZ
fields.description: Reserved, RAZ.

name: JMCR
description: A Jazelle register, which provides control of the Jazelle extension.

fields.name: RAZ/WI
fields.description: Reserved, RAZ/WI.

name: JOSCR
description: A Jazelle register, which provides operating system control of the Jazelle Extension.

fields.name: RAZ/WI
fields.description: Reserved, RAZ/WI.

name: MAIR0
description: Along with MAIR1 , provides the memory attribute encodings corresponding to the possible AttrIndx values in a Long-descriptor format translation table entry for stage 1 translations. AttrIndx[2] indicates the MAIR register to be used: When AttrIndx[2] is 0, MAIR0 is used. When AttrIndx[2] is 1, MAIR1 is used.

fields.name: Attr<n>
fields.description: The memory attribute encoding for an AttrIndx[2:0] entry in a Long descriptor format translation table entry, where: AttrIndx[2:0] gives the value of <n> in Attr<n>. AttrIndx[2] defines which MAIR to access. Attr7 to Attr4 are in MAIR1, and Attr3 to Attr0 are in MAIR0. Bits [7:4] are encoded as follows: Attr<n>[7:4] Meaning 0b0000 Device memory. fields.name: Variant
fields.description: Variant number. Typically, this field is used to distinguish between different product variants, or major revisions of a product.

fields.name: Architecture
fields.description: Architecture version.

values.label: Armv4.
Armv4T.
Armv5 (obsolete).
Armv5T.

Armv6.
Architectural features are individually identified in the ID_* registers.

fields.name: PartNum
fields.description: Primary Part Number for the device. On processors implemented by Arm, if the top four bits of the primary part number are 0x0 or 0x7 , the variant and architecture are encoded differently.

fields.name: Revision
fields.description: Revision number for the device.

name: MPIDR
description: In a multiprocessor system, provides an additional PE identification mechanism.

fields.name: M
fields.description: Indicates whether this implementation includes the functionality introduced by the Armv7 Multiprocessing Extensions.

values.label: This implementation does not include the Armv7 Multiprocessing Extensions functionality.
This implementation includes the Armv7 Multiprocessing Extensions functionality.

fields.name: U
fields.description: Indicates a Uniprocessor system, as distinct from PE 0 in a multiprocessor system.

values.label: Processor is part of a multiprocessor system.
Processor is part of a uniprocessor system.

fields.name: RES0
fields.description: Reserved, RES0 .

fields.name: MT
fields.description: Indicates whether the lowest level of affinity consists of logical PEs that are implemented using an interdependent approach, such as multithreading. See the description of Aff0 for more information about affinity levels.

values.label: Performance of PEs with different affinity level 0 values, and the same values for affinity level 1 and higher, is largely independent.
Performance of PEs with different affinity level 0 values, and the same values for affinity level 1 and higher, is very interdependent.

fields.name: Aff2
fields.description: Affinity level 2. See the description of Aff0 for more information.

fields.name: Aff1
fields.description: Affinity level 1. See the description of Aff0 for more information.

fields.name: Aff0
fields.description: Affinity level 0. The value of the MPIDR .{Aff2, Aff1, Aff0} or MPIDR_EL1 .{Aff3, Aff2, Aff1, Aff0} set of fields of each PE must be unique within the system as a whole.

name: MVBAR
description: When EL3 is implemented and can use AArch32, holds the vector base address for any exception that is taken to Monitor mode. Secure software must program the MVBAR with the required initial value as part of the PE boot sequence.

fields.name: VBA
fields.description: Vector Base Address. Bits[31:5] of the base address of the exception vectors for exceptions taken to this Exception level. Bits[4:0] of an exception vector are the exception offset.

fields.name: Reserved
fields.description: Reserved, see Configurations.

name: MVFR0
description: Describes the features provided by the AArch32 Advanced SIMD and floating-point implementation. Must be interpreted with MVFR1 and MVFR2 . For general information about the interpretation of the ID registers see 'Principles of the ID scheme for fields in ID registers' .

fields.name: FPRound
fields.description: Floating-Point Rounding modes. Indicates whether the floating-point implementation provides support for rounding modes.

values.label: Not implemented, or only Round to Nearest mode supported, except that Round towards Zero mode is supported for VCVT instructions that always use that rounding mode regardless of the FPSCR setting.
All rounding modes supported.

fields.name: FPShVec
fields.description: Short Vectors. Indicates whether the floating-point implementation provides support for the use of short vectors.

values.label: Short vectors not supported.
Short vector operation supported.

fields.name: FPSqrt
fields.description: Square Root. Indicates whether the floating-point implementation provides support for the ARMv6 VFP square root operations.

values.label: Not supported in hardware.
Supported.

fields.name: FPDivide
fields.description: Indicates whether the floating-point implementation provides support for VFP divide operations.

values.label: Not supported in hardware.
Supported.

fields.name: FPTrap
fields.description: Floating-Point Exception Trapping. Indicates whether the floating-point implementation provides support for exception trapping.

values.label: Not supported.
Supported.

fields.name: FPDP
fields.description: Floating-Point Double-Precision. Indicates whether the floating-point implementation provides support for double-precision operations.

values.label: Not supported in hardware.
Supported, VFPv2.
Supported, VFPv3, VFPv4, or Armv8. VFPv3 and Armv8 add an instruction to load a double-precision floating-point constant, and conversions between double-precision and fixed-point values.

fields.name: FPSP
fields.description: Floating-Point Single-Precision. Indicates whether the floating-point implementation provides support for single-precision operations.

values.label: Not supported in hardware.
Supported, VFPv2.
Supported, VFPv3 or VFPv4. VFPv3 adds an instruction to load a single-precision floating-point constant, and conversions between single-precision and fixed-point values.

fields.name: SIMDReg
fields.description: Advanced SIMD registers. Indicates whether the Advanced SIMD and floating-point implementation provides support for the Advanced SIMD and floating-point register bank.

values.label: The implementation has no Advanced SIMD and floating-point support.
The implementation includes floating-point support with 16 x 64-bit registers.
The implementation includes Advanced SIMD and floating-point support with 32 x 64-bit registers.

name: MVFR1
description: Describes the features provided by the AArch32 Advanced SIMD and floating-point implementation. Must be interpreted with MVFR0 and MVFR2 . For general information about the interpretation of the ID registers see 'Principles of the ID scheme for fields in ID registers' .

fields.name: SIMDFMAC
fields.description: Advanced SIMD Fused Multiply-Accumulate. Indicates whether the Advanced SIMD implementation provides fused multiply accumulate instructions.

values.label: Not implemented.
Implemented.

fields.name: FPHP
fields.description: Floating-Point Half-Precision. Indicates the level of half-precision floating-point support.

values.label: Not supported.
Floating-point half-precision conversion instructions are supported for conversion between single-precision and half-precision.
As for 0b0001 , and adds instructions for conversion between double-precision and half-precision.
As for 0b0010 , and adds support for half-precision floating-point arithmetic.

fields.name: SIMDHP
fields.description: Advanced SIMD Half-Precision. Indicates the level of half-precision floating-point support.

values.label: Not supported.
SIMD half-precision conversion instructions are supported for conversion between single-precision and half-precision.
As for 0b0001 , and adds support for half-precision floating-point arithmetic.

fields.name: SIMDSP
fields.description: Advanced SIMD Single-Precision. Indicates whether the Advanced SIMD and floating-point implementation provides single-precision floating-point instructions.

values.label: Not implemented.
Implemented. This value is permitted only if the SIMDInt field is 0b0001 .

fields.name: SIMDInt
fields.description: Advanced SIMD Integer. Indicates whether the Advanced SIMD and floating-point implementation provides integer instructions.

values.label: Not implemented.
Implemented.

fields.name: SIMDLS
fields.description: Advanced SIMD Load/Store. Indicates whether the Advanced SIMD and floating-point implementation provides load/store instructions.

values.label: Not implemented.
Implemented.

fields.name: FPDNaN
fields.description: Default NaN mode. Indicates whether the floating-point implementation provides support only for the Default NaN mode.

values.label: Not implemented, or hardware supports only the Default NaN mode.
Hardware supports propagation of NaN values.

fields.name: FPFtZ
fields.description: Flush to Zero mode. Indicates whether the floating-point implementation provides support only for the Flush-to-Zero mode of operation.

values.label: Not implemented, or hardware supports only the Flush-to-Zero mode of operation.
Hardware supports full denormalized number arithmetic.

name: MVFR2
description: Describes the features provided by the AArch32 Advanced SIMD and floating-point implementation. Must be interpreted with MVFR0 and MVFR1 . For general information about the interpretation of the ID registers see 'Principles of the ID scheme for fields in ID registers' .

As 0b0010 , and Floating-point Round to Integer Floating-point.
As 0b0011 , and Floating-point MaxNum and MinNum.

values.label: This control has no effect on: System register access to implemented trace registers. The behavior of CPACR .TRCDIS and HCPTR .TTA.

Non-secure System register accesses to all implemented trace registers are disabled, meaning: CPACR .TRCDIS behaves as RAO/WI in Non-secure state, regardless of its actual value. HCPTR .TTA behaves as RAO/WI, regardless of its actual value.

fields.name: RES0

fields.name: IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED
fields.description: IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED .

Non-secure access to the Advanced SIMD functionality is disabled, meaning: CPACR .ASEDIS behaves as RAO/WI in Non-secure state, regardless of its actual value. HCPTR .TASE behaves as RAO/WI, regardless of its actual value.

fields.name: cp10
fields.description: Enable Non-secure access to the Advanced SIMD and floating-point features. Possible values of the fields are:

values.label: Advanced SIMD and floating-point features can be accessed only from Secure state. Any attempt to access this functionality from Non-secure state is UNDEFINED . The HCPTR .{TCP11, TCP10} fields behave as RAO/WI, regardless of their actual values.
Advanced SIMD and floating-point features can be accessed from both Security states.

name: PMEVCNTR<n>
description: Holds event counter n, which counts events, where n is 0 to 30.

fields.name: EVCNT
fields.description: Event counter n. Value of event counter n, where n is the number of this register and is a number from 0 to 30. If FEAT_PMUv3p5 is implemented, the event counter is 64 bits and only the least-significant part of the event counter is accessible in AArch32 state: Reads from PMEVCNTR<n> return bits [31:0] of the counter. Writes to PMEVCNTR<n> update bits [31:0] and leave bits [63:32] unchanged. There is no means to access bits [63:32] directly from AArch32 state. If the implementation does not support AArch64, bits [63:32] are not required to be implemented. If FEAT_PMUv3p5 is not implemented, the event counter is 32 bits.

fields.name: evtCount[9:0]
fields.description: Event to count. The event number of the event that is counted by event counter PMEVCNTR<n> . The ranges of event numbers allocated to each type of event are shown in 'Allocation of the PMU event number space' . If FEAT_PMUv3p8 is implemented and PMEVTYPER<n>.evtCount is programmed to an event that is reserved or not supported by the PE, no events are counted and the value returned by a direct or external read of the PMEVTYPER<n>.evtCount field is the value written to the field. Arm recommends this behavior for all implementations of FEAT_PMUv3 . Otherwise, if PMEVTYPER<n>.evtCount is programmed to an event that is reserved or not supported by the PE, the behavior depends on the value written: For the range 0x0000 to 0x003F , no events are counted and the value returned by a direct or external read of the PMEVTYPER<n>.evtCount field is the value written to the field. If FEAT_PMUv3p1 is implemented, for the range 0x4000 to 0x403F , no events are counted and the value returned by a direct or external read of the PMEVTYPER<n>.evtCount field is the value written to the field. For other values, it is UNPREDICTABLE what event, if any, is counted and the value returned by a direct or external read of the PMEVTYPER<n>.evtCount field is UNKNOWN . UNPREDICTABLE means the event must not expose privileged information.

name: PMXEVCNTR
long_name: Performance Monitors Selected Event Count Register
description: Reads or writes the value of the selected event counter, PMEVCNTR<n> . PMSELR .SEL determines which event counter is selected.

fields.name: PMEVCNTR<n>
fields.description: Value of the selected event counter, PMEVCNTR<n> , where n is the value stored in PMSELR .SEL. If FEAT_PMUv3p5 is implemented, the event counter is 64 bits and only the least-significant part of the event counter is accessible in AArch32 state: Reads from PMXEVCNTR return bits [31:0] of the counter. Writes to PMXEVCNTR update bits [31:0] and leave bits [63:32] unchanged. There is no means to access bits [63:32] directly from AArch32 state. If the implementation does not support AArch64, bits [63:32] are not required to be implemented. If FEAT_PMUv3p5 is not implemented, the event counter is 32 bits.

fields.name: IDC
fields.description: Input Denormal cumulative floating-point exception bit. This bit is set to 1 to indicate that the Input Denormal floating-point exception has occurred since 0 was last written to this bit. How VFP instructions update this bit depends on the value of the IDE bit. Advanced SIMD instructions set this bit if the Input Denormal floating-point exception occurs in one or more of the floating-point calculations performed by the instruction, regardless of the value of the IDE bit.

fields.name: RES0
fields.description: Reserved, RES0 .

fields.name: IXC
fields.description: Inexact cumulative floating-point exception bit. This bit is set to 1 to indicate that the Inexact floating-point exception has occurred since 0 was last written to this bit. How VFP instructions update this bit depends on the value of the IXE bit. Advanced SIMD instructions set this bit if the Inexact floating-point exception occurs in one or more of the floating-point calculations performed by the instruction, regardless of the value of the IXE bit. The criteria for the Inexact floating-point exception to occur are different in Flush-to-zero mode. For more information, see 'Flush-to-zero' .

fields.name: UFC
fields.description: Underflow cumulative floating-point exception bit. This bit is set to 1 to indicate that the Underflow floating-point exception has occurred since 0 was last written to this bit. How VFP instructions update this bit depends on the value of the UFE bit. Advanced SIMD instructions set this bit if the Underflow floating-point exception occurs in one or more of the floating-point calculations performed by the instruction, if FPSCR.UFE is 0 or if Flush-to-zero is enabled. The criteria for the Underflow floating-point exception to occur are different in Flush-to-zero mode. For more information, see 'Flush-to-zero' .

fields.name: OFC
fields.description: Overflow cumulative floating-point exception bit. This bit is set to 1 to indicate that the Overflow floating-point exception has occurred since 0 was last written to this bit. How VFP instructions update this bit depends on the value of the OFE bit. Advanced SIMD instructions set this bit if the Overflow floating-point exception occurs in one or more of the floating-point calculations performed by the instruction, regardless of the value of the OFE bit.

fields.name: DZC
fields.description: Divide by Zero cumulative floating-point exception bit. This bit is set to 1 to indicate that the Divide by Zero floating-point exception has occurred since 0 was last written to this bit. How VFP instructions update this bit depends on the value of the DZE bit. Advanced SIMD instructions set this bit if the Divide by Zero floating-point exception occurs in one or more of the floating-point calculations performed by the instruction, regardless of the value of the DZE bit.

fields.name: IOC
fields.description: Invalid Operation cumulative floating-point exception bit. This bit is set to 1 to indicate that the Invalid Operation floating-point exception has occurred since 0 was last written to this bit. How VFP instructions update this bit depends on the value of the IOE bit. Advanced SIMD instructions set this bit if the Invalid Operation floating-point exception occurs in one or more of the floating-point calculations performed by the instruction, regardless of the value of the IOE bit.
